channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Hallucinations to agents: Here’s how AI language models evolved in 2024
item.description: I research the intersection of artificial intelligence, natural language processing, and human reasoning as the director of the Advancing Human and Machine Reasoning lab at the University of South Florida.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Creatives Find AI Tools Useful for Work, Survey Reveals
item.description: A survey conducted by Monotype found that 91% of creatives find AI tools useful for their work, challenging the myth that they are 'anti-AI'.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 5 network automation startups to watch
item.description: To meet digital transformation initiatives, businesses across the economy are seeking ways to automate network operations. According to a report from Mordor Intelligence, the network automation market should top $25 billion by the end of 2024, expanding to more than $71 billion in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 3D-printed or ‘ghost’ guns are a growing global security threat
item.description: Police investigating the shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on Dec. 4, 2024, have announced that the suspected assailant had used a 3D-printed gun. Several high-profile crimes in recent years have involved this kind of homemade, or partially homemade, weapon.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Top 5 mistakes that could expose your financial data to cybercriminals
item.description: There are common habits and oversights that could leave your financial information vulnerable to cybercriminals, according to Kurt the CyberGuy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Arm awaits verdict in high-stakes licensing suit against Qualcomm
item.description: Arm is waiting to see if it has won a battle over licensing of its microprocessor designs to Qualcomm that has cast doubt over the immediate future of some of the chip maker’s products.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New iPhone auto-restart feature locks encrypted data after extended inactivity
item.description: Apple's iOS 18.1 Inactivity Reboot automatically reboots your iPhone if it hasn't been used or unlocked for more than three days, providing better data protection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Smart policing revolution: How Kazakhstan is setting a global benchmark
item.description: In the era of digital transformation, public safety stands at a critical crossroads. Law enforcement agencies globally are under increasing scrutiny to enhance transparency, efficiency, and trust within their communities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DCSA details new plan for next-gen background investigation system
item.description: DCSA's new plan for NBIS includes a 36-month product roadmap. The goal is to retire legacy background investigation systems, including PIPs, by fiscal 2027. The post DCSA details new plan for next-gen background investigation system first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 2024 obituaries: Notable Bay Area residents who died
item.description: SF Giants stars Willie Mays and Orlando Cepeda, tech pioneer Susan Wojcicki and music legend Phil Lesh are among those who left a mark on the Bay Area in their lifetimes.
item.dc:creator: Bay Area News Group, The Mercury News

item.title: Tabnine code assistant now flags unlicensed code
item.description: Looking to minimize IP liability for generative AI output, Tabnine’s AI coding assistant now checks code for licensing restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Doomscrolling among teens leads to growing 'brain rot' concerns
item.description: The word brain rot increased by 240% in 2024 as health concerns over constant online scrolling among teens grows
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4
